rulings.Case Title: PROTAX LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. SHREE JALARAM TOWN HOUSE MOTEL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Reasonable Attorney'S Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declarations Of Michael Middleton, Chris Middleton And Arthur A. Small Ii In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2025, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PROTAX LLC's, Motion For An Award Of Reasonable Attorney's Fees. Off calendar. All real property-related law and motion matters are to be calendared in Department 501 under SF Local Rule 2.7B. This motion will need to be re-noticed in department 501. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK).

rulings.Case Title: TODD BEAUGRAND VS. DOE DEFENDANT 1 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Serve Complaint; For In Camera Review Of Plaintiff'S Certificates Of Merit Pursuant To Ccp Section 340.1(I)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2025, Line 2, PLAINTIFF TODD BEAUGRAND's Motion To Serve Complaint; For In Camera Review Of Plaintiff's Certificates Of Merit Pursuant To CCP Section 340.1(I). Plaintiff Todd Beaugrand's motion to serve complaint as to DOES 1-5 is granted as to all five DOES. Per CCP 340.1(h), after an in camera review based solely on attorney and mental health practitioner certificates of merit as to DOES 1-5, the court finds that there is reasonable and meritorious cause for the filing of this action against each of DOES 1-5. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK).

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD R. VS. DOE 1, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A First Amended Complaint; Declaration Of Alex M. Valenzuela
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2025, Line 3, PLAINTIFF RICHARD R.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Richard R.'s motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is denied without prejudice to the plaintiff refiling his motion for leave to file a first amended complaint if the currently scheduled trial date of October 6, 2025 is continued, discovery is reopened as to plaintiff's proposed request for punitive damages, and defendant Doe 3 has sufficient time to file a demurrer and motion for summary adjudication (possibly on shortened time agreed to by the plaintiff) as to the plaintiff's proposed request for punitive damages. With the imminent trial date and even more imminent discovery deadlines, notwithstanding California's strong public policy favoring amendments to pleadings, granting this motion would result in significant prejudice to Doe 3. That prejudice could be eliminated by a trial continuance, reopening of discovery, and sufficient time for Doe 3 to file a demurrer and motion for summary adjudication. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK).

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Ali A. Aalaei
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2025, Line 8, PLAINTIFF PAUL HENRY's Motion For A Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions. 1 - Plaintiff Paul Henry's motion for a protective order is denied as moot in light of the ruling on defendant Emmanuel Kampouris' motion to compel Mr. Henry's deposition. No monetary sanctions are awarded to either Mr. Henry or Mr. Kampouris because both parties share at least some of the responsibility for the failure to agree on a date for the deposition of Mr. Henry. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: PAUL HENRY VS. ERIC YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Paul Henry'S Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2025, Line 8, DEFENDANT EMMANUEL KAMPOURIS' Motion To Compel Plaintiff Paul Henry's Deposition. 2 - Defendant Emmanuel Kampouris's motion to compel plaintiff Paul Henry's deposition is granted. Mr. Henry's deposition will take place at the Stento Court Reporting Office in Salt Lake City on any week day selected by Mr. Kampouris from September 3, 2025 through and including September 24, 2025. Mr. Kampouris' counsel must notify Mr. Henry's counsel and counsel for all other defendants of the date selected for Mr. Henry's deposition no later than 8am on August 25, 2025 and include that date in the proposed order submitted for this motion. Given the imminence of the trial date and pendency of the summary judgment motions, Mr. Kampouris has good cause to take the deposition of Mr. Henry and that deposition must take precedence over any other conflicting matters scheduled by Mr. Henry and his counsel. No monetary sanctions are awarded to Mr. Kampouris because both he and Mr. Henry share at least some of the responsibility for the failure to agree on a date for the deposition of Mr. Henry. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS LUDWIGSEN ET AL VS. ALI SHAFIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Admission And For Monetary Sanctions Of $3,000 Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2025, Line 9, PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS LUDWIGSEN, JINYUAN LI's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Admission And For Monetary Sanctions Of $3,000 Against Defendant. 2 - Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses from defendant Ali Shafie to plaintiffs' set one requests for admission and for $3,000 in monetary sanctions is granted. No later than September 16, 2025 Mr. Shafie must serve verified code-compliant responses without objections to plaintiffs' first set of requests for admission and he and his counsel must pay $3,000 in monetary sanctions to plaintiffs. Plaintiffs' first set of discovery requests seek discoverable information and do not pose an undue burden on Mr. Shafie. All of Mr. Shafie's objections lack merit and are stricken. Mr. Shafie's responses to plaitniffs' first set of discovery are evasive and contrary to both the letter and spirit of California's civil discovery rules. Reasonable monetary sanctions of $3,000 are awarded because Mr. Shafie and his counsel acted without substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK).

The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK).

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO OROZCO GONZALEZ ET AL VS. SESO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Transfer, Coordination, And Consolidation Of Noncomplex Cases Filed In Different Courts Pursuant To Ccp 403, 404.1, And Crc Rule 03-500 Rule3-500
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 25, 2025, Line 11, PLAINTIFF EDGAR GAMBINO REYES, JAIRO GURROLA, MAURO DIAZ SANTILLAN, JAIRO GURROLA, MIGUEL MENDEZ NAVARRO's Motion For Transfer, Coordination, And Consolidation Of Noncomplex Cases Filed In Different Courts Pursuant To CCP 403, 404.1, And CRC Rule 03-500. Plaintiffs' motion for transfer, coordination and consolidation of three non-complex cases is denied without prejudice to refiling a motion covering all cases arising out of the March 22, 2024 incident in Shasta County and after the San Diego Superior Court rules on a similar motion that was noticed to be heard by the Seso Defendants on September 12, 2025. Although the papers filed by the parties identify four other California state court cases (the Arceo, Vilamil, Navarro, and Ramirez cases) that were pending as of the date this motion was filed and all of them arise out of the March 22, 2024 incident in Shasta County, inexplicably those four cases are not part of this motion, arguably undermining the rationale for this motion. Moreover, this court is concerned about the unseemliness of a similar motion pending in San Diego Superior Court at the time this motion was filed, which could result in inconsistent decisions. This court believes that the appropriate course of action is for the first-filed of the two motions to be resolved first and, if appropriate, obviate the need for a motion to be heard in the San Francisco Superior Court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK).
